provisions.description: Prohibits expenditure of funds by any DoD entity unless it complies with the Buy American Act (chapter 83 of title 41, USC). provisions.description: Prohibits use of funds to establish a field operating agency or to pay personnel transferred from a headquarters activity if they remain at the headquarters location. Waivers allowed on a case-by-case basis if the Secretary certifies it will reduce personnel or financial requirements. Exemptions for NIP-funded agencies, Army IED/biometrics agencies, and Air Force mortuary affairs agency.
provisions.title: VIII
provisions.raw_text:
SEC. 8044. 
(a) Except as provided in subsections (b) and (c), none of the funds made available by this Act may be used— (1) to establish a field opera
notes: Subsection (a) contains two prohibitions: (1) establishing field operating agencies, (2) paying personnel transferred from headquarters who remain at headquarters
Subsection (b) allows case-by-case waivers by SecDef or Service Secretaries with certification to appropriations committees
Subsection (c) exempts NIP-funded agencies, Army IED agency, Army biometrics agency, and Air Force Mortuary Affairs agency

provisions.description: Prohibits conversion of DoD civilian functions to contractor performance unless a public-private competition shows contractor cost is less by at least 10% of personnel costs or $10M, and the contractor does not gain advantage by reducing health insurance. Exceptions for Javits-Wagner-O'Day Act entities, Indian tribes, and Native Hawaiian organizations. Does not apply to depot contracts.
provisions.title: VIII
provisions.raw_text: SEC. 8045. (a) None of the funds appropriated by this Act shall be available to convert to contractor performance an activity or function of the Depart
notes: Three-part test in subsection (a): public-private competition, cost savings threshold (10% or $10M), and no health insurance advantage
Subsection (b) exempts contracts with Javits-Wagner-O'Day entities, Indian tribes (51%+ ownership), and Native Hawaiian Organizations
Subsection (b)(2) exempts depot contracts under 10 U.S.C. 2469 and 2474
Subsection (c) credits compliant conversions toward competitive/outsourcing goals

provisions.description: Requires that any Executive order or Presidential memorandum issued or revoked during FY2024 be accompanied by a written statement from the OMB Director on budgetary impact, including a narrative summary, 5-year impact on mandatory and discretionary obligations and outlays by agency, and 5-year impact on revenues. Emergency orders may have the statement issued within 15 days. The requirement applies only to Presidential memoranda with estimated regulatory cost exceeding $100,000,000.
deadlines: concurrent with issuance (or within 15 days for emergency orders)
provisions.title: II
provisions.raw_text:
SEC. 202. 
(a) During fiscal year 2024, any Executive order or Presidential memorandum issued or revoked by the President shall be accompanied by a writ
notes: Subsection (a) establishes the general requirement for OMB cost statements.
Subsection (b) specifies three required elements: narrative summary, 5-year mandatory/discretionary impact by agency, and 5-year revenue impact.
Subsection (c) provides 15-day grace period for national emergency orders.
Subsection (d) limits the memorandum requirement to those with regulatory cost exceeding $100,000,000.

provisions.description: Freezes pay rates during calendar year 2024 for the Vice President, Executive Schedule employees, political appointees in the Senior Executive Service at or above Executive Schedule Level IV, and other political appointees paid at or above Level IV. Rates are frozen at the levels in effect on December 31, 2023, under the prior year's equivalent provision. Permits pay increases only upon authorized movement to a different covered position with higher-level duties. Applies through the end of the last biweekly pay period beginning in CY2024. Takes effect on the first applicable pay period beginning on or after January 1, 2024.

Subsection (c): Chiefs of mission and ambassadors at large subject to (b)
Subsection (d): Noncareer SES and political limited-term/emergency SES at or above ES Level IV
Subsection (e): Other political appointees at or above ES Level IV, with exceptions for GS, Foreign Service, 3161 appointees, and positions equivalent to GS-15 or below
Subsection (f): Savings clause for non-political appointees
Subsection (g): Exception for SES basic pay election under 5 USC 3392(c)
Subsection (h): Exception for Senior Foreign Service pay election under FSA section 302(b)
Subsection (i): Exception for movement to higher-level covered position
Subsection (j): New appointees to covered positions subject to Dec 31, 2023 rates
Subsection (k): Bar extends through end of biweekly pay period straddling CY2024-2025
Subsection (m): Effective date on first applicable pay period on or after Jan 1, 2024

Applies to pilots using more than 10 FTEs or obligating $5,000,000 or more.

Subsection (f) excludes IT procurement testing/evaluation and non-Federal financial assistance recipient activities

notes: Standard first-class travel prohibition

provisions.title: V
provisions.raw_text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this Act, none of the funds appropriated or otherwise made available by this Act may be used to pay award or inc
notes: Notwithstanding clause overrides any other provision in this Act

provisions.description: Prohibits use of funds to maintain or establish a computer network unless it blocks viewing, downloading, and exchanging of pornography. Exception for law enforcement agencies and entities carrying out criminal investigations, prosecution, or adjudication activities.

provisions.raw_text: (a) None of the funds made available in this Act may be used to maintain or establish a computer network unless such network blocks the viewing, downlo
notes: Subsection (b) provides law enforcement exception at Federal, State, tribal, territorial, and local levels

provisions.description: Prohibits use of funds by a Federal law enforcement officer to facilitate the transfer of an operable firearm to an individual if the officer knows or suspects the individual is an agent of a drug cartel, unless U.S. law enforcement continuously monitors or controls the firearm at all times.
provisions.title: V
provisions.raw_text: None of the funds made available in this Act may be used by a Federal law enforcement officer to facilitate the transfer of an operable firearm to an i
notes: Rider enacted in response to Operation Fast and Furious; recurring provision since FY2012

provisions.description: Prohibits travel to or attendance of more than 50 employees of a single DHS component at a single international conference unless the Secretary determines it is in the national interest and provides 10-day advance notification. Total conference cost to DHS may not exceed $500,000. Virtual attendees without travel are excluded from the 50-employee cap.
provisions.title: V
provisions.raw_text: (a) None of the funds made available in this Act may be used to pay for the travel to or attendance of more than 50 employees of a single component of
notes: International conference defined as a conference outside the U.S. attended by U.S. and foreign government, IO, or NGO representatives
$500,000 cap per conference for total DHS costs (subsection c)
Virtual attendees without travel excluded from cap (subsection d)
50-employee limit is per DHS component, not department-wide

provisions.description: Prohibits use of funds made available in this Act to reimburse any Federal department or agency for its participation in a National Special Security Event.

provisions.raw_text: None of the funds made available in this Act may be used to reimburse any Federal department or agency for its participation in a National Special Secur

provisions.description: Prohibits obligation of DHS funds for structural pay reform or new position classification affecting more than 100 FTEs or costing more than $5,000,000/year until 30 days after notification to Congress, with exceptions for changes proposed in the President's budget and not explicitly denied.

notes: Applies to this and any other Act providing DHS funds

Exception for changes proposed in President's budget that were not explicitly denied

provisions.description: Requires agencies receiving funds in this Act to post on their public website any report required by the Committees on Appropriations, subject to exceptions for national security and proprietary information, and only after a 45-day waiting period after providing the report to the committees.
provisions.title: V
provisions.raw_text: (a) Any agency receiving funds made available in this Act shall, subject to subsections (b) and (c), post on the public website of that agency any rep
notes: Exceptions for reports that compromise homeland/national security or contain proprietary information
45-day waiting period after committee receipt before public posting

provisions.description: Authorizes use of Operations and Support funding for minor procurement, construction, and improvements, defining 'minor' as unit cost of $250,000 or less for personal property and $2,000,000 or less for real property.
provisions.title: V
provisions.raw_text: (a) Funding provided in this Act for ''Operations and Support'' may be used for minor procurement, construction, and improvements. (b) For purposes of
notes: Applies to all DHS Operations and Support accounts
Personal property threshold: $250,000; real property threshold: $2,000,000

provisions.description: Continues the authority provided by section 532 of Division F of P.L. 114-113 regarding prohibition on funding for any position designated as Principal Federal Official, but actually continues section 532 of the DHS Appropriations Act, 2018 (P.L. 115-141) regarding primary and secondary schooling of dependents during fiscal year 2024.

provisions.raw_text: The authority provided by section 532 of the Department of Homeland Security Appropriations Act, 2018 (Public Law 115–141) regarding primary and secon
notes: Extends existing authority from P.L. 115-141 for schooling of dependents to FY2024

provisions.description: Prohibits use of DHS funds to prevent Members of Congress or their designated employees from entering DHS detention or housing facilities for oversight purposes, or to make temporary modifications that alter what is observed. Does not require prior notice from Members, though 24-hour advance notice may be required for designated employees.
provisions.title: V
provisions.raw_text: (a) None of the funds appropriated or otherwise made available to the Department of Homeland Security by this Act may be used to prevent any of the fo
notes: Covers facilities operated by or for DHS used to detain or house aliens
Members of Congress need not provide prior notice
24-hour advance notice may be required for designated employees

provisions.description: Prohibits use of funds to place restraints on pregnant women or those in post-delivery recuperation in DHS custody, with exceptions for serious flight risk, immediate serious threat of harm, or when medical professional determines therapeutic restraints are appropriate. Specifies safest and least restrictive restraints must be used; prohibits restraints during active labor/delivery and prohibits certain restraint positions.
provisions.title: V
provisions.raw_text: (a) Except as provided in subsection (b), none of the funds made available in this Act may be used to place restraints on a woman in the custody of th
notes: Covers transport, detention facilities, and outside medical facilities
Exceptions: serious flight risk, immediate serious threat of harm, therapeutic restraints per medical professional
Absolute prohibition on restraints during active labor/delivery
Prohibits face-down, four-point, on-back, or pregnancy-area restraint belt positions

provisions.description: Prohibits use of funds to destroy any document, recording, or record pertaining to death, potential sexual assault or abuse, or allegations of abuse/criminal activity/disruption committed by an individual in DHS custody. Records must be made available to charged/segregated/punished individuals upon request.

notes: Records must be made available to individuals who have been charged, placed in segregation, or punished as a result of an allegation

provisions.description: Applies the prohibition from section 519 of Division F of P.L. 114-113 regarding a prohibition on funding for any position designated as a Principal Federal Official to any Federal funds in the same manner as in that Act.

notes: Extends prohibition from P.L. 114-113 to apply to any Federal funds under this Act

cross_references.description: Prohibition on funding for Principal Federal Official positions

provisions.description: Requires the Under Secretary for Management of Homeland Security to submit to the Appropriations Committees a report on unfunded priorities for DHS and each component for which discretionary funding would be classified as budget function 050, not later than 10 days after the President's budget submission. Report must include summary description, objectives, account information, and staffing details for each unfunded priority.
deadlines: 10 days after the date on which the budget of the President for a fiscal year is submitted to Congress
provisions.title: V
provisions.raw_text: (a) Not later than 10 days after the date on which the budget of the President for a fiscal year is submitted to Congress pursuant to section 1105(a)
notes: Budget function 050 = National Defense
Defines 'unfunded priority' as a requirement not funded in the budget that is necessary for operational/contingency plans
Covers requirements emerging since budget formulation or needed to sustain prior-year investments

provisions.description: Requires the Secretary of Homeland Security to notify congressional leadership and specified committees within 10 days of a Presidential determination to evaluate and initiate protection for a former/retired Government official or employee ('covered individual'). Requires notification 15 days before extending or 30 days before terminating protection. Requires quarterly reports detailing each covered individual, scope, and cost of protection. Notifications may be classified.
deadlines: 10 days after determination to initiate protection
45 days after enactment and quarterly thereafter
provisions.title: V
provisions.raw_text: (a) Not later than 10 days after a determination is made by the President to evaluate and initiate protection under any authority for a former or reti
notes: Notifications must include threat assessment, scope, anticipated cost and duration
May be classified in consultation with DNI or FBI Director
Quarterly reporting required

provisions.description: Prohibits DHS agencies from submitting initial project proposals to the Technology Modernization Fund unless they concurrently notify the Appropriations Committees, submit a copy of the proposal, and provide a detailed analysis of how the funding would supplement or supplant existing budget requests. Funds from the TMF are not available for obligation until 15 days after a report is transmitted to the Committees.
provisions.title: V
provisions.raw_text: (a) None of the funds provided to the Department of Homeland Security in this or any prior Act may be used by an agency to submit an initial project p
notes: Applies to funds from this or any prior Act
15-day waiting period for TMF funds after committee notification
Report must include full proposal, interagency agreement, repayment plan

cross_references.target: Section 1078, Subtitle G, Title X, National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2018 (P.L. 115-91)

provisions.description: Requires the Secretary of Homeland Security, within 60 days of any FY2025 budget submission that assumes revenues or proposes reductions based on unenacted user fee proposals, to provide the Appropriations Committees with specific reductions in proposed discretionary budget authority commensurate with the assumed revenues in the event those proposals are not enacted prior to October 1, 2024.
deadlines: 60 days after any budget submission for DHS for fiscal year 2025

provisions.raw_text: Within 60 days of any budget submission for the Department of Homeland Security for fiscal year 2025 that assumes revenues or proposes a reduction from
notes: Contingency planning requirement for unenacted user fee proposals

provisions.description: Prohibits DHS from using federal funds to enter into procurement contracts, MOUs, cooperative agreements, grants, loans, or guarantees with entities identified under section 1260H of the FY2021 NDAA (Chinese military companies list) or any subsidiary thereof.

provisions.description: Prohibits use of funds in this or any other Act to transfer, release, or assist in the transfer or release to or within the United States, its territories, or possessions of Khalid Sheikh Mohammed or any other non-citizen Guantanamo Bay detainee held on or after June 24, 2009.

provisions.description: Requires the Secretary of Homeland Security to develop bimonthly estimates of noncitizens anticipated to arrive at the southwest border, with demographic breakouts, independent validation, and inclusion in budget materials. Suspends reprogramming/transfer authority under SEC. 503 if estimates are not provided.
provisions.title: V
provisions.raw_text: SEC. 538. (a) The Secretary of Homeland Security (in this section referred to as the ''Secretary'') shall, on a bimonthly basis beginning immediately af
notes: Estimates must cover current and following fiscal year

Must undergo independent validation and verification review
Must be used to inform DHS policy planning and budgeting
Must be shared with HHS Secretary, Attorney General, Secretary of State, and Appropriations Committees
Subsection (d): SEC. 503 reprogramming/transfer authority suspended if estimates not provided

cross_references.target: SEC. 503 of Division C
cross_references.description: Reprogramming and transfer authority suspended if estimates not provided

provisions.description: Amends Section 7 of the Fair Labor Standards Act to add subsection (s) exempting from overtime provisions, for a 2-year period after a major disaster, employees who adjust or evaluate disaster-related claims, subject to minimum weekly compensation of $591.00 and other conditions. Effective on date of enactment.
provisions.title: I
provisions.raw_text: (a) Section 7 of the Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938 (29 U.S.C. 207) shall be applied as if the following text is part of such section:
notes: Adds new subsection (s) to FLSA Section 7
2-year exemption period following a major disaster declaration by any State or Federal agency
Minimum weekly compensation threshold of $591.00 or Secretary-established amount, whichever is greater
Covers claims adjusters/evaluators not affiliated with underwriting, selling, or marketing insurance
Does not affect exemption under section 13(a)(1) of FLSA
Subsection (b): effective on date of enactment

cross_references.target: Section 7 of the Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938 (29 U.S.C. 207)
cross_references.description: Adds overtime exemption subsection (s) for disaster claims adjusters

provisions.description: Allows employers in the seafood industry with granted H-2B petitions to bring nonimmigrants into the U.S. at any time during a 120-day period from the start date without filing another petition. Crossings after 90 days require new local labor market assessment and offer to qualified U.S. workers. Secretary of Labor shall not consider this as staggering in violation of 20 CFR 655.20(d).
provisions.title: I
provisions.raw_text:
(a) 
Flexibility with respect to the crossing of H–2B nonimmigrants working in the seafood industry
(1) 
In general
Subject to paragraph (2), if a petiti
notes: 120-day window for initial entry without new petition
After 90 days, employer must re-assess local labor market via newspaper listings (2 Sundays) and DOL Electronic Job Registry
Must offer job to equally or better qualified U.S. worker who applies and is available
H-2B defined as aliens under section 101(a)(15)(H)(ii)(B) of the INA

cross_references.description: Staggering rule from which seafood employers are exempted

provisions.description: Sets the prevailing wage for H-2B program as the greater of (1) the actual wage level paid by the employer to similarly situated employees, or (2) the prevailing wage level for the occupational classification in the geographic area. Secretary shall accept private wage surveys even when OES data are available unless the methodology is not statistically supported.

provisions.raw_text: The determination of prevailing wage for the purposes of the H–2B program shall be the greater of—(1) the actual wage level paid by the employer to ot
notes: Two-prong prevailing wage test: actual employer wage vs. occupational prevailing wage in geographic area
Private wage surveys accepted alongside Occupational Employment Statistics survey data
Secretary can reject private survey only if methodology and data are not statistically supported

provisions.description: Prohibits use of any funds in this Act to enforce the definition of corresponding employment in 20 CFR 655.5 or the three-fourths guarantee rule in 20 CFR 655.20, or any references thereto. provisions.description: Applies new statutory text to the Act creating the Department of Labor, authorizing the Secretary of Labor to employ law enforcement officers or special agents to provide protective security detail for the Secretary, immediate family members under specified conditions, and senior officers at public events under threat. Authorizes carrying firearms, warrantless arrests, protective intelligence, coordination with local law enforcement, and criminal investigations. Officers must comply with Attorney General and Secretary-prescribed guidelines.

provisions.description: Prohibits use of funds appropriated in this Act for any abortion or for health benefits coverage that includes coverage of abortion (Hyde Amendment).
provisions.title: V
provisions.raw_text: (a) None of the funds appropriated in this Act, and none of the funds in any trust fund to which funds are appropriated in this Act, shall be expended
notes: Subsection (a) prohibits expenditure for any abortion
Subsection (b) prohibits expenditure for health benefits coverage that includes abortion
Subsection (c) defines 'health benefits coverage' as the package of services covered by a managed care provider or organization
Standard Hyde Amendment language

provisions.description: Establishes exceptions to the Hyde Amendment abortion funding prohibition: allows funding for abortions resulting from rape or incest, or where the woman's life is endangered. Clarifies that state/local/private funds may still be used. Also prohibits discrimination against health care entities that do not provide, pay for, or refer for abortions.
provisions.policy_area: Abortion funding exceptions and conscience protections
provisions.title: V
provisions.raw_text:
(a) The limitations established in the preceding section shall not apply to an abortion— 
(1) if the pregnancy is the result of an act of rape or inces
notes: Subsection (a) provides rape/incest and life-of-the-mother exceptions
Subsection (b) clarifies state/local/private funds (excluding Medicaid matching) are not restricted
Subsection (c) clarifies managed care providers can offer abortion coverage with state funds
Subsection (d) prohibits discrimination against health care entities that decline to provide/pay for/refer for abortions (Weldon Amendment)

cross_references.target: SEC. 506
cross_references.description: Provides exceptions to the abortion funding prohibition in the preceding section

provisions.description: Prohibits use of funds for creation of human embryos for research purposes or for research in which human embryos are destroyed, discarded, or subjected to risk of injury or death greater than allowed under 45 CFR 46.204(b) and section 498(b) of the Public Health Service Act (Dickey-Wicker Amendment).

(a) None of the funds made available in this Act may be used for— (1) the creation of a human embryo or embryos for research purposes; or 
(2) research
notes: Standard Dickey-Wicker Amendment language

provisions.description: Prohibits funds under titles III through VI from providing assistance under new bilateral agreements unless the agreement exempts US assistance from taxation or provides for reimbursement. Includes de minimis exception, reprogramming of withheld funds, waiver authority based on effective reimbursement arrangements or foreign policy interests, and implementation and definitional provisions.

Subsection (g)(2): 'Taxes and taxation' includes value added taxes and customs duties but excludes individual income taxes on local staff.

provisions.description: Prohibits funds under part I of the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 from being used for: (1) abortions as a method of family planning; (2) motivating or coercing any person to practice abortions; (3) involuntary sterilization as family planning; (4) coercing or providing financial incentives for sterilization; (5) biomedical research related to abortions or involuntary sterilization as family planning. Also prohibits obligation or expenditure for any country or organization if the President certifies the use would violate these provisions.

provisions.description: Requires funds under titles III through V to be made available in amounts designated in allocation tables in the explanatory statement. Designated amounts for foreign countries and international organizations serve as the section 653(a) report amounts. Allows up to 10 percent deviation (5 percent for Global Programs under ESF) upon determination and reporting that deviation is necessary for significant, exigent, or unforeseen events or exceptional national security circumstances. Deviations subject to prior consultation and notification. Reports required per committee reports and explanatory statement.
provisions.title: VII
provisions.raw_text: Subject to subsection (b), funds appropriated by this Act under titles III through V shall be made available in the amounts specifically designated in t
notes: Heading: ALLOCATIONS AND REPORTS
Subsection (a): Allocation tables — funds must follow explanatory statement tables; designated country/org amounts serve as the 653(a) report.
Subsection (b): Authorized deviations — up to 10% deviation allowed; exceeding 10% requires written justification for significant/exigent/unforeseen events or national security.
Subsection (c): Deviations for 653(a) amounts only after submission of that report.

Subsection (d): Exceptions — does not apply to expired funds or minimum funding requirements; deviation authority does not apply to Global Health Programs table; Global Programs under ESF limited to 5% deviation.
Subsection (e): Reports required per House Report 118-146, Senate Report 118-71, and explanatory statement.
Subsection (f): International Disaster Assistance and Migration and Refugee Assistance excluded from country designation amounts unless specifically designated.

provisions.description: Amends the Special Drawing Rights Act to prohibit the United States from engaging in voluntary SDR exchange transactions with member countries whose governments have committed genocide or repeatedly provided support for international terrorism, unless authorized by Congress. Directs US Executive Director at each international financial institution to oppose financial assistance to and SDR transactions with such governments. Includes a presidential waiver with reporting requirement.

Paragraph (3) prohibits SDR transactions with genocide perpetrators and state sponsors of terrorism
Paragraph (4) directs US Executive Director to oppose assistance and SDR transactions with such governments
Paragraph (5) provides presidential waiver with reporting to House Financial Services and Senate Foreign Relations committees

provisions.description: Repeals paragraphs (3) through (5) of section 6(b) of the Special Drawing Rights Act (the SDR transaction prohibitions added by subsection (a)) effective 10 years after date of enactment.

provisions.description: Makes major amendments to the Price-Anderson Act: (a) extends the Act by 40 years from December 31, 2025 to December 31, 2065; (b) increases the liability limit for DOE contractors from $500,000,000 to $2,000,000,000 in two subsections; (c) extends the reporting deadline from December 31, 2021 to December 31, 2061; (d) amends the definition of nuclear incident by removing certain exclusionary language.

Subsection (b)(2): Increases liability cap in subsection e.(4) from $500,000,000 to $2,000,000,000
Subsection (c): Extends reporting deadline from December 31, 2021 to December 31, 2061

Liability amounts are caps on DOE contractor indemnification, not federal spending
